title: The Mushroom Becomes Hope
description: On pages 6–9, this part focuses on the mushroom becomes hope, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: The Mushroom Becomes Hope. Chopper tries to save Hiriluk with the mushroom he believes is medicine, turning love and ignorance into tragedy. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: So? What did you come here for today?

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: Couldn't be to say a word of thanks, right?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: And you still have 2 weeks left to live...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Actually, I have a rogue-

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: I refuse.

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: I haven't even told you what it is!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Well none of your requests ever pay out for me.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The hell?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Poor

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Is this dirt or something?

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: That, my dear Kureha,

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Is the result of over 30 years of my medical research!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: It's the panacea that will cure even the sick heart of this country!

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: I see... Congrat- relations then.

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: That's quite the pointless 30 years you've lived. So... why're you giving this to me?

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: I don't have enough of that dust nor any time left.

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: So I want you to make the cherry blossoms bloom in my stead.

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: What!? Why the hell do I have to such a ridiculous th-

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: And one more thing!

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: I want you to teach Chopper medicine! He wants to become a doctor!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Enough is enough! Do you think you're in any position to demand things from me!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: He may be a naive Reindeer,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: But I know he can become a great doctor one day!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: He's a good- natural kid...!

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: He even risked his life to make me some medicine...! So please, teach him how to become a doctor!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Get out of my house! You've tested my patience for far too long!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Demanding ME to continue your ridiculous research and look after your weird pet!?

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: You should know better than to think I'd take pity on you just because you're on the verge of death!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Now get out!

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Yes...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: I do know better than anyone how you'll act...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: I'll be counting on you then...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Eh eh eh eh... i know just how kind one needs to be,

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: To pursue a life of saving others... doctors are all good- natural people in the end.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Take good care,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: of Chopperr...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What do you mean the entire MD-20 has collapsed! They're the only doctors in this entire kingdom!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Rope- way #4

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Just who's going to look after the sick and injured then!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: At least give us some medicine!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Yelling at us won't change anything! Doctors are human too, it's not strange for them to fall sick.

summary: This part highlights the mushroom becomes hope and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
